title: Authors, writers and translators
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. (Based on 29 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Consult with authors and editors regarding manuscript changes and suggestions; read, evaluate and edit manuscripts or other materials submitted for publication, and confer with authors regarding changes in content, style or organization, or publication.

Proofread, edit, and revise translated materials; check translations of technical terms and terminology to ensure that they are accurate and remain consistent throughout translation revisions; correct or record omissions, errors, or inconsistencies found; mark copy to indicate and correct errors in type, arrangement, grammar, punctuation, or spelling, using standard printers' marks.

Write original content, such as headlines, cutlines, captions, and cover copy; develop ideas or material for columns or commentaries by analyzing and interpreting news, current issues, or personal experiences; write columns, editorials, commentaries, or reviews that interpret events or offer opinions; conduct interviews with people who can provide information about a story; arrange interviews with people who can provide information about a story; gather information and develop perspectives about news subjects through research, interviews, observation, and experience.

Translate messages simultaneously or consecutively into specified languages, orally or by using hand signs, maintaining message content, context, and style, as much as possible; listen to speakers' statements to determine meanings and prepare translations, using electronic listening systems as necessary; read written materials, such as legal documents, scientific works, or news reports, and rewrite material into specified languages; compile terminology and information to be used in translations, including technical terms such as those for legal or medical material; read, evaluate and edit translated materials; discuss translation requirements with clients and determine any fees to be charged for services provided.

Follow ethical codes that protect the confidentiality of information; engage in professional development activities, such as taking continuing education classes and attending or participating in conferences, workshops, professional meetings, and associations; evaluate materials to determine outdated or unused items to be discarded; maintain records and files of work and revisions.

Analyze developments in the specific field to determine need for revisions in previously published materials and the development of new material; identify and resolve conflicts related to the meanings of words, concepts, practices, or behaviors; interpret data from various sources, including reference materials, to ensure translation accuracy; observe production, developmental, and experimental activities to determine operating procedure and detail; and select material most pertinent to presentation and organize this material into appropriate formats.

Archive documents, conduct research, and read copy using the internet and various computer programs; compile lists of books, periodicals, articles, and audio-visual materials on particular subjects; code, classify and catalog books, publications, films, audio-visual aids, and other library materials based on subject matter or standard library classification systems; maintain inventory of audio-visual equipment and troubleshoot problems with audio-visual equipment.

Direct and train library staff in duties such as receiving, shelving, researching, cataloging, and equipment use; supervise daily library operations, including budgeting, planning, and personnel activities such as hiring, training, scheduling, and performance evaluations; confer with colleagues, faculty, and community members and organizations to conduct informational programs and determine library services to offer; plan and teach classes on topics such as information literacy, library instruction, and technology use.

Check original texts or confer with authors to ensure that translations retain the content, meaning, and feeling of the original material; travel with or guide tourists who speak another language; adapt translations to students' cognitive and grade levels, collaborating with educational team members as necessary; train and supervise other translators or interpreters; route proofs with marked corrections to authors, editors, typists, or typesetters for correction or reprinting; check books in and out of the library; run environmental documents, such as environmental assessments or environmental impact statements.

Engage clients in discussions about their needs and requirements for writing projects; prepare necessary documents to obtain planned project approvals or permits; evaluate vendor products and performance, negotiate contracts, and place orders; and prepare or deliver speeches to further public relations objectives.

Compile and maintain databases; develop, maintain, and troubleshoot information access aids, such as databases, annotated bibliographies, web pages, electronic pathfinders, software programs, and online tutorials.

Remove tasks related to audio-visual production, such as setting up audio-visual equipment, purchasing advertising space, and public relations that do not specifically pertain to writing and translation.
Select local, state, national, and international news items received from wire services, based on assessment of items' significance and interest value.
Arrange for copyright permissions and follow appropriate procedures to get copyrights for completed work.

Assign topics, events and stories to individual writers or reporters for coverage and interview and hire writers and reporters or negotiate contracts, royalties, and payments for authors or freelancers.

Direct the policies and departments of newspapers, magazines and other publishing establishments and oversee publication production, including artwork, layout, computer typesetting, and printing, ensuring adherence to deadlines and budget requirements.
Develop story or content ideas, considering reader or audience appeal and plan the contents of publications according to the publication's style, editorial policy, and publishing requirements.
Review and approve proofs submitted by composing room prior to publication production and read copy or proof to detect and correct errors in spelling, punctuation, and syntax.

Prepare, rewrite, and edit copy to improve readability, or supervise others who do this work, and revise written material to meet personal standards and to satisfy needs of clients, publishers, directors, or producers.

Write text, such as stories, articles, editorials, or newsletters, and write fiction or nonfiction prose, such as short stories, novels, biographies, articles, descriptive or critical analyses, and essays.
Participate in the development or implementation of online marketing strategy and execute or manage social media campaigns to inform search marketing tactics.
Confer with clients, editors, publishers, or producers to discuss changes or revisions to written material and collaborate with other writers on specific projects.

Conduct market research analysis to identify search query trends, real-time search and news media activity, popular social media topics, electronic commerce trends, market opportunities, or competitor performance, and gather data on competitors and analyze their prices, sales, and method of marketing and distribution.
Collaborate with marketing staff to integrate and complement marketing strategies across multiple sales channels and propose online or multiple-sales-channel campaigns to marketing executives.
Create content strategies for digital media and write articles, bulletins, sales letters, speeches, and other related informative, marketing and promotional material.

Attend staff conferences to provide management with information and proposals concerning the promotion, distribution, design, and pricing of company products or services and interact with clients to develop strategies for their careers, explaining actions taken on their behalf.
Keep informed of industry trends and deals and evaluate advertising trends, consumer surveys, and other data regarding marketing of goods and services to determine the best way to promote products.

Conduct research to obtain factual information and authentic detail, using sources such as newspaper accounts, diaries, and interviews and confer with engineers, architects, owners, contractors, and subcontractors on changes and adjustments to cost estimates.

Develop factors such as themes, plots, characterizations, psychological analyses, historical environments, action, and dialogue to create material, and choose subject matter and suitable form to express personal feelings and experiences or ideas, or to narrate stories or events.
Prepare works in appropriate format for publication, and send them to publishers or producers, and collect fees, commissions, or other payments, according to contract terms.
Revise copy based on client feedback and make necessary changes to advertising copy based on client discussions to ensure alignment with their vision.

Collect historical cost data to estimate costs for current or future products and prepare cost and expenditure statements and other necessary documentation at regular intervals for the duration of a project.
Conduct audits or interviews to evaluate potential clients and negotiate with managers, promoters, union officials, and other persons regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations.
Set up cost monitoring and reporting systems and procedures, and establish and maintain processes for conducting negotiations and tendering.
Conduct special studies to develop and establish standard hour and related cost data or to reduce cost.
Need visit sites to gather information about access, drainage, topography, and availability of utility services.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to new and existing customers, explaining how specific types of advertising will promote their products or services.
Maintain and develop account bases while processing all correspondence and paperwork related to accounts, and collect payments due for advertising work.
Locate and contact potential clients to offer advertising services and provide estimates of costs for advertising products or services.
Train others in fraud detection and prevention techniques and provide analytical support to researchers or traders on issues such as valuations or data.
Research financial products or analytics, and produce written summary reports of financial research results, interpreting the results of financial analysis procedures.

Develop core analytical capabilities or model libraries using advanced statistical, quantitative, or econometric techniques, and devise independent models or tools to verify analytical system results.
Negotiate with responsible parties to recover losses due to fraud, and advise businesses on improving fraud detection.
Assess cost-effectiveness for products, projects, or services, tracking actual costs relative to bids as the project develops.
Prepare evidence for presentation in court and testify regarding investigation findings.
Maintain knowledge of current events and trends to inform the assessment of sustainability initiatives, and prepare reports summarizing initiatives' effectiveness.
Create marketing or outreach materials, and promote sustainability initiatives through appropriate channels.
Develop or implement risk assessment models, and document, and ensure communication of key risks relevant to sustainability or environmental policies.
Design and implement systems to monitor validity of risk assessments and provide technical or administrative support for sustainability programs or issues.
Compose descriptions of merchandise for online storefronts, ensuring optimization for search engines and corresponding with customers to address queries or complaints.
Prepare or organize promotional material and generate scenarios for various potential business disruptions.
Calculate financial metrics such as revenue and expenses while conducting field surveillance to gather necessary information for reports.
Attend professional meetings and participate in training to stay current with developments in disaster recovery and business continuity.
Establish, maintain, or test call trees for effective communication during disaster and ensure compliance with government regulations and applicable codes.
Develop or review specifications for the design or construction of security systems and prepare, maintain, or update related documentation.
Design, implement, or establish requirements for security systems, including policies and practices, to ensure adequate security relating to alarm response and access card use.
Perform risk analyses and assess the nature and level of physical security threats to develop appropriate security countermeasures.
Monitor the performance of contractors, employees, and project team members to ensure compliance, quality standards, and that goals are met.
Conduct interviews to identify responsible parties for security breaches and resolve customer complaints regarding services or products.
Plan and prepare work schedules, assign employees to specific duties, and hire, train, and evaluate personnel.
Budget and schedule security design work and prepare documentation for case reports or court proceedings.
Communicate with project personnel and stakeholders to coordinate activities, submit deliverables, and ensure adherence to project requirements and quality standards.
Create project plans and prepare and submit budget estimates or progress reports, including tracking costs incurred by project staff.
Develop conceptual designs of security systems and provide recommendations for system design and integration.
Monitor security systems, including fire and intruder detection systems, and inspect security design features for compliance with standards or regulations.
Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities with other departments.
